name: No-Coins Draninus
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears green felt linens and an olive vest - typical lower-class clothing, though in somewhat better condition than most beggars possess.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: fortunate, resourceful, content, observant, practical
Role: No-Coins Draninus is a beggar living in the Talos Plaza District of the Imperial City. Despite his nickname, he has managed to carve out a relatively comfortable existence compared to other beggars, thanks to his location in the wealthiest district of the city. He can provide useful information to those willing to pay for it.
Home Location Information: No-Coins Draninus has no proper home, but his bedroll is located in the narrow alleyway behind Ulen Athram's house in the Talos Plaza District. Unlike most beggars, he owns a few possessions - three bottles of cheap wine, a food bag, and a sack containing minor belongings.
Daily Routine: Draninus wakes at 6am and immediately heads to the large statue in the center of Talos Plaza District, where he spends the entire day begging for food and gold. At 6pm, he actively seeks out food and consumes it where he stands. At 8pm, he returns to his bedroll and wanders the alley for two hours before going to sleep at 10pm.
Life in Talos Plaza: Being a beggar in the wealthiest district of the Imperial City has made Draninus's life somewhat easier than that of his counterparts elsewhere. The affluent residents and visitors are more likely to have coins to spare, and his nickname "No-Coins" may be somewhat ironic given his relatively comfortable circumstances. He doesn't suffer as much as other beggars - he has his wine, his belongings, and a reliable spot at the central statue.
Personality - The Fortunate Beggar: Draninus has found his niche in Talos Plaza and appears content with his situation. Unlike beggars in poorer districts who must constantly move between locations hoping for scraps, he has claimed the prime begging spot at the district's central statue and holds it all day. His possession of three bottles of cheap wine and other belongings suggests he manages to acquire enough to afford small luxuries. Like many beggars, he keeps his eyes and ears open, gathering information that can be sold to those seeking knowledge of the city's underworld. His relatively stable existence has given him a practical, almost businesslike approach to his position on society's margins.
author: Bethesda
